live_path_build_2026_05_29.CONTAMINATION_CORRECTION_2026_05_29: Claude's prior 'wire M3 to the frozen gold rubric' was a CONTAMINATION ERROR. The gold rubric (gold_rubrics_pathB.md / rubric_v3_frozen.json) is the EXTERNAL SCORER'S pre-registered answer key — feeding it INTO POLARIS's own generation/D8 gate would be teaching-to-the-test and would void the pre-registration. TWO SEPARATE SYSTEMS: (1) POLARIS's 4-role pipeline = POLARIS's OWN internal per-claim verification (Mirror->Sentinel->Judge) + D8 release gate; its required-elements/must-cover/severity must come from POLARIS's OWN completeness machinery (config/completeness_checklists + scope) + config/architecture/d8_release_policy.yaml S0 defaults, NEVER the gold rubric. (2) The BENCHMARK SCORER = an EXTERNAL dual Claude+Codex §-1.1 audit that reads each system's REPORT against the frozen rubric, populates a Ledger (Claim verdicts + Coverage rows), reconciles (reconcile.py conservative-MAX), scores (score_run.score_one, lanes: hard_fail==0 + coverage>=0.70), aggregates (aggregate_systems.render_final_report). This scorer is ALREADY BUILT (scripts/dr_benchmark/{ledger_schema,score_run,reconcile,aggregate_systems}.py + rubric_v3_frozen.json) and is SEPARATE from POLARIS internals. CRUX: the claim->rubric-element coverage mapping is a MANUAL/LLM AUDITOR step (NOT automated in code) — the auditor populates Coverage rows. So the head-to-head needs: POLARIS PRODUCES the 5 reports (live run, blind to the rubric) -> external dual audit scores all 3 -> aggregate.

GREEN: 5 Qs + 10 competitor files (SHA-matched) + frozen rubric (pins match) + scoring math (judge-side, refuses invalid/non-reconciled/SHA-drift) + contamination boundary CLEAN (zero rubric reads in src/ or sweep) + M1 transport. Gate-A reconfirmed GREEN in real env (318 tests, dry run PASS, exit 0; the workflow agent's overall_pass=false was a transient temp/ACL PermissionError, not a regression). RULINGS: (a) keep POLARIS blind to gold rubric — CONFIRMED; (b) FourRoleEvaluationInputs MUST come from POLARIS NATIVE config/scope_templates/<domain>.yaml + config/completeness_checklists/<domain>.yaml + native claim->element map + deterministic native severity map; fail closed if no native mapping; NEVER the gold rubric; (c) NO OpenRouter-verifier shortcut (lock pins verifier roles to vast_self_host*; generator stays OpenRouter); offline-mocked e2e authorizes only a tiny 1-question paid CANARY, not the full 5-question spend; (d) NOT_READY now. ORDERED no-spend work: 1 keep Gate-A green; 2 M2 self-host serving (Cohere bf16 + Granite + Qwen fp8 + PG_*_BASE_URL + keys + health + identity); 3 M3 Gate-B caller (construct transport + build inputs from native scope/completeness + PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE + inject); 4 self-host preflight branch (validate PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL + served identity, no OpenRouter resolution for vast_self_host; generator stays OpenRouter); 5 M4 self-host served==pinned (pin endpoint/model, consume _pathb_served.endpoint in assert_post_run, fixtures); 6 M5 evaluator_agrees writeback at real assembly point; 7 update stale Path-B docs/tests gen+eval->4-role; 8 promote lock to locked (checkpoints + operator signature); 9 ONE offline-mocked golden-Q end-to-end (report->4-role manifest->evaluator_agrees->pathB PASS->fixture reconciled ledger->score_run->aggregate). THEN spend: 1-question paid canary first; full 5-Q only after canary PASS.
